mnemonics: Commenting Code is Like Leaving Breadcrumbs - It Guides Future Developers (and Yourself) Back Through Your Logic
keyPoints: Comments are non-executable notes for developers to explain code purpose and logic
Single-line comments (//) and multi-line comments (/* */) are the two primary syntax types
Good comments explain 'why' the code exists, not just 'what' it does
commonMistakes: Over-commenting obvious code, which creates noise and maintenance overhead
Writing outdated comments that no longer match the actual code functionality
Using comments to excuse bad code instead of refactoring to make the code self-documenting
